key_points: Democracy is a form of government in which rulers are elected by the people and are accountable to them
Democracy requires institutional arrangements such as free elections, political equality and constitutional rule
The legitimacy of democratic governments comes from the consent of the governed
Democracy is based on the idea that citizens have equal political rights and opportunities to participate
Democratic governments must respect fundamental rights including freedom of expression and association
Political competition through elections ensures accountability of leaders
Democracy improves the quality of decision making because decisions are made after discussion and consultation
Democracy provides mechanisms for peaceful resolution of social and political conflicts
Democracy enhances the dignity of citizens by recognizing them as equal participants in governance
Democracy allows correction of mistakes because citizens can replace leaders through elections
examples: Pakistan example illustrates how military influence can weaken democratic institutions
China example shows how lack of electoral competition limits democratic choice
Mexico example demonstrates how dominant party systems can weaken democracy
India demonstrates a functioning multi-party democratic system
Student council elections in schools reflect democratic practices
Community meetings where people discuss local issues show democratic participation
formulas: Democracy = Government elected by people + Political equality + Rule of law
Accountability = Leaders answerable to citizens through elections
Political participation = Citizens actively engage in governance
Democratic legitimacy = Authority derived from consent of the people
exam_tips: Explain clearly the four features of democracy with examples
Use Pakistan, China and Mexico case studies in answers
Highlight arguments in favour of democracy such as accountability and better decision making
Explain criticisms of democracy such as delays in decision making
Link democratic principles with real-life examples when writing answers
